content.problemStatement:
There is an N×NN\times NN×N grid, with rows numbered 111 to NNN from top to bottom and columns numbered 111 to NNN from left to right. The cell at the intersection of the iii-th row and jjj-th column is denoted (i,j)(i, j)(i,j).

Chef is standing at position (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1​,y1​) of this grid. He wants to reach position (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2​,y2​).

To achieve this, he can take either horizontal or vertical steps.
That is, from cell (x,y)(x, y)(x,y), he can move to one of (x−1,y),(x+1,y),(x,y−1),(x,y+1)(x-1, y), (x+1, y), (x, y-1), (x, y+1)(x−1,y),(x+1,y),(x,y−1),(x,y+1).

However, Chef **does not need to stay within the grid**, that is, he can choose to go outside it if he so wishes.

The cost of a horizontal/vertical step is 000 if **both** the starting and ending square lie outside the grid, and 111 otherwise.

Formally, suppose Chef takes one step from (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) to (x′,y′)(x', y')(x′,y′). Then,


If (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) lies within the grid, the cost of this step is 111
If (x′,y′)(x', y')(x′,y′) lies within the grid, the cost of this step is 111
If both the above don't hold, the cost of this step is 000
Find the **minimum cost** to reach (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2​,y2​) from (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1​,y1​).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of
test cases.
Each test case consists of one line of input containing five space-separated integers NNN, x1x_1x1​, y1y_1y1​, x2x_2x2​, and y2y_2y2​.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum cost of traveling from (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1​,y1​) to (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2​,y2​).
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains five space-separated integers N, x1, y1, x2, y2.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the minimum cost on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: both cells are inside, Manhattan distance = 3, but going outside costs 2 → answer 2.
Test case 2: both inside, Manhattan distance = 2 → answer 2.
Test case 3: both inside, Manhattan distance = 1 → answer 1.
The points are the same, so the cost is 0.
Both points are outside the grid, so the cost is 0.

solution.approach: The cost of a step is 0 only when both the start and end cells are outside the N×N grid; otherwise it is 1. Therefore the whole path cost depends only on whether the start and end positions are inside the grid. • If both positions are outside, Chef can move entirely outside and pay 0. • If exactly one of them is inside, any path must cross the border at least once, incurring a cost of 1. • If both are inside, Chef can stay inside the grid. The cheapest way is to move directly using Manhattan distance, but any step inside costs 1, so the total cost is the Manhattan distance capped at 2 (because moving more than two steps can be replaced by going outside for a cost of 2). The answer is: 0 if both outside, 1 if exactly one inside, otherwise min(Manhattan distance, 2). This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- >0){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());long N=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());long x1=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());long y1=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());long x2=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());long y2=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());if(x1==x2 && y1==y2){sb.append(0).append('\n');continue;}boolean in1=(x1>=1 && x1<=N && y1>=1 && y1<=N);boolean in2=(x2>=1 && x2<=N && y2>=1 && y2<=N);int ans;if(in1 && in2){long man=Math.abs(x1-x2)+Math.abs(y1-y2);ans=(int)(man<2?man:2);}else if(in1 || in2){ans=1;}else{ans=0;}sb.append(ans).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
